slides.title: Problem Statement
slides.notes: Job applications are time-intensive: 8-12 minutes each for Easy Apply, most are poor fits. Manual evaluation is inconsistent. No persistent learning across sessions. This system automates the entire pipeline with quality-first scoring.

slides.title: Architecture Overview
slides.notes: Three-layer architecture: Claude browser agent (scrape + score + apply), file-based IPC bridge (400ms poll), Python watcher (execute + persist). No external APIs, no cloud dependencies. SQLite WAL mode for concurrent reads. Everything checkpointed for crash recovery.

slides.title: Scoring Engine Deep Dive
slides.notes: 5-factor scoring on 100-point scale. Skills overlap is highest weight (30pt) because direct skill match is the strongest signal. Taxonomy Jaccard (25pt) catches domain alignment even without exact keyword match. TF-IDF cosine (20pt) provides semantic similarity. Title and seniority round it out. 65-point threshold validated: only 2/25 jobs qualified, both genuine DLP/security roles.

slides.title: Results & Metrics
slides.notes: First run: 25 jobs evaluated in NYC Metro DLP search. Score distribution clusters around 30-45 (correct rejections). Two qualifying matches: Zscaler DLP consultant (74.0) and Blue Team principal lead (68.0). Zero false positives. Application throughput: ~45 seconds vs 8-12 minutes manual.

slides.title: Knowledge Bank & Learning
slides.notes: Q&A bank stores normalized question/answer pairs with confidence scores and usage tracking. Lookup-first strategy ensures consistent answers. Every new question encountered during applications gets stored. Over time, the system requires less generation and more retrieval — compounding efficiency.

slides.title: Roadmap
slides.notes: Expand search profiles: cloud security, security architect, director-level roles. Multi-session knowledge transfer. Score weight tuning based on application outcomes. Integration with CCEM APM for real-time monitoring. Potential: multi-candidate scaling.
